summary: Gaba Printing is a family-owned, full-service commercial printer in Hackensack, NJ serving the NY/NJ tri-state area. They produce marketing collateral, large-format signage, vehicle wraps, custom apparel, and specialty items, and build branded web-to-print portals so corporate teams can order brand-consistent materials. Their customers are local small-to-mid business owners, marketing managers, contractors, and real estate agents who want a reliable local printer that gets quality right the first time.

campaigns.name: Welcome & First-Order Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Get a new contact or first-time account to their 'aha' — confidently uploading a file, approving the proof, and holding a great first print run — while establishing Gaba as the trusted local expert.
campaigns.trigger: New storefront account created or first quote/order submitted; send email 1 within seconds.
campaigns.segment: New contacts and first-time buyers; branch later by product interest (collateral, large-format, apparel, corporate portal).
campaigns.rationale: Gaba has no welcome sequence today, yet the first print run is exactly where trust is won or lost — guiding the proof-approval step removes the biggest source of first-order anxiety and plants the seed for reorders and the portal.

emails.purpose: Warm family-owned welcome, set expectations on proof + turnaround, and drive the single most important first action: starting an order or uploading artwork for a proof.
emails.subject: Welcome to Gaba — let's get your first print right
emails.preview: Upload your file, approve your proof, and we handle the rest. A real person reads every reply.
keyPoints: Welcome from Darin and the Gaba family — three generations of printing in the tri-state, not a faceless online print factory
Walk through the simple path: upload your file, we send a proof, you approve, we print and deliver on deadline
Reassure on the #1 first-order fear — every job gets a proof you approve before it prints, so the color and layout come out exactly as you envisioned
Invite a direct reply or call to (201) 470-4382 if they're unsure about file setup or specs

campaigns.name: Quote-to-Order Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a free quote requester who hasn't ordered into a paying customer by removing the commodity-printer objection and proving local quality and turnaround reliability.
campaigns.trigger: Free quote requested but no order placed within 2 days.
campaigns.segment: Quote requesters with no completed order; branch by requested product (e.g., business cards, banners, apparel, portal).
campaigns.rationale: Quote requesters who go silent are Gaba's clearest lost revenue, and the moment they hesitate is exactly when 'why not just use Vistaprint?' wins — this email answers that objection head-on while the project is still warm.

emails.purpose: Re-engage the warm quote, directly counter the commodity-printer comparison with quality, local accountability, and reliable turnaround, and make placing the order one click.
emails.subject: Still deciding on your [product]? Here's what big-box printers can't do
emails.preview: Real proof approval, local quality control, and a deadline we actually hit — not a roll of the dice.
emails.cta: Approve your quote & place the order
keyPoints: Acknowledge the quote and the real choice they're weighing — Gaba vs. a cheap online printer like Vistaprint or GotPrint
Counter the objection: a local team that proofs your job, catches color and layout issues before they print, and stands behind the result
Reliability angle — materials ready when you need them for the event, launch, or campaign driving the deadline
Low-friction close: reply with a yes or click to confirm; Darin is reachable directly for anything custom

campaigns.name: Reorder Reminder & Account Expansion
campaigns.objective: Turn one-time buyers into repeat customers and grow order value — prompt timely restocks and expand into adjacent products (apparel, signage) or a corporate portal.
campaigns.trigger: Time-since-last-order threshold reached (e.g., 45–90 days, tuned by product type) or a known seasonal restock window.
campaigns.segment: Past buyers; branch by health — repeat/multi-product buyers get the portal/expansion offer, single-order buyers get a straight reorder nudge.
campaigns.rationale: The brand's biggest stated leak is one-time buyers who never return and default back to commodity printers — there's no reorder nudge today, and repeat-order rate is the primary target metric.

emails.purpose: Remind the customer it's time to restock their last order, make reordering effortless, and surface one relevant expansion (matching apparel/signage or a team portal).
emails.subject: Running low on [last product]? Reorder in two clicks
emails.preview: Your artwork is on file — we can have the next run printed and out the door fast.
keyPoints: Reference their specific last order and that their approved artwork is already on file — no setup, no re-proofing hassle
Make the reorder one action and reassure on the same quality and turnaround they got last time
Soft expansion nudge — pair their collateral with matching banners, apparel, or signage, or set up a branded portal so their whole team can order on-brand
Family-owned reminder that they're a valued local customer, not an account number

campaigns.name: Lapsed Customer Win-Back
campaigns.objective: Reactivate customers who have gone quiet, remind them of the value of a trusted local printer, and clean the list of true non-responders.
campaigns.trigger: No order or engagement for 6+ months.
campaigns.segment: Dormant past buyers; separate gentle branch for those who only ever placed one order.
campaigns.rationale: Gaba has no lapsed-customer campaign, so dormant buyers silently drift to cheaper printers; a low-cost win-back can recover 5–15% before they're gone for good.

emails.purpose: Open the win-back with a warm 'we miss you,' remind them what made Gaba worth choosing, and give a frictionless reason to come back for their next project.
emails.subject: It's been a while — your tri-state print shop is still here
emails.preview: Same family, same quality, ready whenever your next project is.
keyPoints: Warm, no-guilt re-open from Darin — they trusted Gaba before and the local team would love to print for them again
Remind of the core value: proofed quality, reliable turnaround, and a real person who picks up the phone
Lower the barrier — invite them to send the next job or just reply with what they need a quote on
Optional gentle incentive or priority turnaround on their next order to make returning easy

campaigns.name: The Print Shop Insider (Value Newsletter)
campaigns.objective: Stay top-of-mind across the long, irregular print buying cycle and build authority as the local print expert — without discounting.
campaigns.segment: All engaged contacts; suppress active onboarding and route the long-dormant to Win-Back instead.
campaigns.rationale: Print needs are sporadic, so the customer who isn't ordering this month still decides where to go next quarter — a steady, useful monthly touch keeps Gaba the obvious choice and softens the price-shopping reflex.

emails.purpose: Deliver the flagship monthly edition — one genuinely useful print/branding tip plus a seasonal idea — that builds habit and trust with a single soft CTA.
emails.subject: 3 things that make a banner actually get noticed
emails.preview: A quick tip from the shop floor — plus what local businesses are printing this season.
keyPoints: Lead with one practical, behind-the-shop tip (e.g., how to design large-format so it reads from across the street, or file specs that avoid blurry prints)
Tie to a timely, seasonal angle relevant to tri-state businesses (events, grand openings, holiday signage)
Showcase a quick local mini-example or recent project to demonstrate range and quality
Single soft CTA to explore the product or request a quote, with a reply invitation to keep it human
